workflow_name: QBR Transcript -> Health + Whitespace + Renewal Risk
user_message: I need you to process this escalation email from Humongous Insurance. Multiple stakeholders are involved -- it's complex.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: QBR preparation Discussion - Humongous Insurance
Date: 2024-01-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance), David Chen (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on QBR preparation from our last session.

[00:06:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:11:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:14:00] David Chen (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:17:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around budget approval.

[00:19:00] David Chen (Microsoft): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:24:00] Michael Petrov (Humongous Insurance): On the QBR preparation front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Humongous Insurance: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF45-N trap (Whitespace hypotheses generated per QBR when only some are warranted); produces complete Health delta assessment, whitespace hypotheses, renewal risk with confidence, action distribution wi
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF45-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF45-N failure mode (Whitespace hypotheses generated per QBR when only some are warranted)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct QBR preparation decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF45-N -- Whitespace hypotheses generated per QBR when only some are warranted
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
